card_description: Osiris is a historical figure referenced in Philip K. Dick's Exegesis. Appears in 2 Exegesis passages. Context: "Dick explores the "Lived Myth" (Joseph Campbell) and "Ma'at" (Right Order) as keys to his 1974 experience."

linked_segments.summary: Dick explores the "Lived Myth" (Joseph Campbell) and "Ma'at" (Right Order) as keys to his 1974 experience. He identifies the "Xerox Letter" as a ritual repudiation of materialism and a transformation
linked_segments.title: U_Claudia_28.txt

linked_segments.summary: Dick identifies the "Dead and Reborn God" experience as the emotional core of the Fish-Christian underground. He concludes that the universe is "inside-out" or "backward," with God (the Void) found in
